title: The Financial Toxicity of Universal SGLT2i in HF
text:

Confidence intervals (95%) were calculated using bootstrap-estimated standard errors (2000 replicates) and log-transformation to account for the right-skewed distribution of health care costs. Estimates marked with an asterisk (*) are considered statistically unreliable per the 2017 National Center for Health Statistics standards (due to high relative standard error, insufficient effective sample size, or low degrees of freedom).

Notes: Estimates apply to all US adults with heart failure. Financial toxicity is defined as annual out-of-pocket health care expenditures (including premiums) exceeding 20% of post-subsistence income. ‘Universal SGLT2i’ assumes 100% adherence through insurance; cash pathways apply a rational buyer minimum of insured vs cash prices. CIs for proportions are Korn-Graubard beta intervals based on bootstrap standard errors (2000 replicates). Cost CIs use bootstrap standard errors with log-transformation. Estimates marked with an asterisk (*) are considered statistically unreliable per the 2017 National Center for Health Statistics standards (due to high relative standard error, insufficient effective sample size, or low degrees of freedom).

Notes: Financial toxicity is defined as annual out-of-pocket health care expenditures (including premiums) exceeding 20% of post-subsistence income (income remaining after food, clothing, transportation, shelter, and other necessities). ‘Universal SGLT2 Inhibitor Adherence’ assumes 100% adherence to guideline-directed therapy. ‘Inflation Reduction Act Implementation’ applies a $2,100 annual pharmacy cap to the Medicare Part D portion. CIs calculated using Korn-Graubard beta intervals based on bootstrap standard errors (2000 replicates). Estimates marked with an asterisk (*) are considered statistically unreliable per the 2017 National Center for Health Statistics standards (due to high relative standard error, insufficient effective sample size, or low degrees of freedom).

Notes: Survey-weighted annualized SGLT2i out-of-pocket distributions are shown as median, IQR, and 10th-90th percentile range by insurance type. Baseline SGLT2i General Adult Population = all MEPS adults (age >=18) currently using SGLT2i with observed daily costs (broad donor pool). Baseline SGLT2i Heart Failure Cohort = observed current SGLT2i users within the final insured heart failure analytic cohort prior to PMM. Universal SGLT2i Heart Failure Cohort = final insured heart failure cohort after universal-use assignment, combining observed and PMM-imputed SGLT2i daily costs.
